skill.name: context-degradation
skill.description: Recognize patterns of context failure: lost-in-middle, poisoning, distraction, and clash
skill.summary: Detect and mitigate language model context degradation patterns including lost-in-middle, poisoning, distraction, and clash
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 20 potential issues including external_commands, network, and weak cryptographic algorithms. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES: the 'external_commands' detections are YAML token count examples with backtick formatting; 'network' is a legitimate GitHub URL in metadata; 'weak cryptographic algorithm' is a pattern matching error triggered by the word 'degradation'; 'system reconnaissance' falsely triggers on 'multi-source retrieval'. This skill is pure educational documentation about LLM context degradation with no executable code.

high_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at lines 169, 176, 179. These are YAML token count examples (turn_20: 60000 tokens) used as documentation, not actual shell commands.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Code examples are clearly marked with markdown fences and show token counts as documentation, not executable commands

high_findings.title: False Positive: Network Security Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Hardcoded URL' at line 4. This is a legitimate GitHub source URL in the skill metadata, not a security vulnerability.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: URL is a standard GitHub reference link in YAML frontmatter metadata, not a network request or data exfiltration

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at 16 locations. Pattern matcher triggers on the word 'degradation' (appears as 'deg' in scanning patterns). No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill discusses 'context degradation' in LLMs - no cryptographic algorithms present. Pattern matcher error on 'degradation' substring

high_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'System reconnaissance' at line 92. Content discusses 'multi-source retrieval' in the context of information retrieval research, not system reconnaissance.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line discusses data retrieval from multiple sources in academic context, not system reconnaissance

content.value_statement: Language models exhibit predictable performance degradation as context length increases. This skill helps diagnose lost-in-middle, poisoning, distraction, and clash patterns to build more reliable AI systems.
actual_capabilities: Identify lost-in-middle patterns where information in the middle of context receives less attention
Detect context poisoning where errors compound through repeated reference
Recognize context distraction when irrelevant information overwhelms relevant content
Diagnose context clash when accumulated information directly conflicts
Apply the Four-Bucket Approach: Write, Select, Compress, and Isolate strategies
Use architectural patterns like observation masking and sub-agent isolation
limitations: This skill provides diagnostic knowledge but does not modify model internals
Effectiveness depends on the specific LLM being used and its context handling
Cannot fix fundamental context window limitations of underlying models
Mitigation strategies may increase computational overhead

use_cases.title: Debug Agent Failures
use_cases.description: When an AI agent produces incorrect or irrelevant outputs during long conversations, use this skill to identify whether context degradation is the root cause
use_cases.target_user: AI developers and engineers debugging production issues

use_cases.title: Design Resilient Systems
use_cases.description: Architect systems that handle large contexts reliably by applying the Four-Bucket Approach and architectural patterns described in this skill

use_cases.title: Evaluate Context Choices
use_cases.description: Make informed decisions about context engineering for production systems by understanding degradation thresholds and mitigation strategies
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads evaluating AI implementation strategies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Degradation Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this conversation for context degradation patterns. The conversation has grown to over 50000 tokens. Look for signs of lost-in-middle, poisoning, distraction, or clash.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking a long conversation for degradation

prompt_templates.title: Lost-in-Middle Diagnosis
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the attached context and identify if critical information is buried in the middle. The task requires information from the middle section but outputs are incorrect.

prompt_templates.title: Context Poisoning Recovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this context for signs of poisoning. Symptoms include degraded output quality, tool misalignment, and persistent hallucinations despite corrections. What steps can recover?
prompt_templates.scenario: Detecting and recovering from context poisoning

prompt_templates.title: Architectural Pattern Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Given a system that processes 200K+ token contexts with multiple independent tasks, recommend which Four-Bucket strategies (Write, Select, Compress, Isolate) to apply and why.

output_examples.input: Conversation has 60000 tokens. Agent started producing incorrect summaries after turn 20.
output_examples.output: Analysis: Context degradation detected. The lost-in-middle phenomenon is likely causing the agent to miss key information from the middle of context. Recommendation: Apply compaction to summarize earlier context, or restructure to place critical info at edges.

output_examples.input: User asks about code from turn 1, but agent refers to wrong implementation from turn 15.
output_examples.output: Analysis: Context clash detected. Multiple implementations exist in context with conflicting details. Recommendation: Use explicit versioning and mark conflicts for clarification before proceeding.

best_practices: Place critical information at the beginning or end of context where attention is highest
Monitor context length and performance correlation during development
Implement compaction triggers before degradation becomes severe
anti_patterns: Assuming longer context always improves performance
Loading all retrieved documents without relevance filtering
Allowing context to grow indefinitely without segmentation

faq.question: What is the lost-in-middle phenomenon?
faq.answer: The lost-in-middle phenomenon is when models demonstrate U-shaped attention curves. Information at the beginning and end of context receives reliable attention, while information in the middle suffers from dramatically reduced recall accuracy.

faq.question: How does context poisoning occur?
faq.answer: Context poisoning occurs when hallucinations, errors, or incorrect information enters context and compounds through repeated reference. Once poisoned, context creates feedback loops that reinforce incorrect beliefs.

faq.question: What is the Four-Bucket Approach?
faq.answer: The Four-Bucket Approach includes: Write (save context outside the window), Select (pull relevant context through retrieval), Compress (reduce tokens through summarization), and Isolate (split context across sub-agents).

faq.question: Do larger context windows always help?
faq.answer: No. Larger contexts can create new problems including performance degradation curves, disproportionate cost increases, and cognitive bottleneck issues where models struggle to maintain quality across many tasks.

faq.question: How do I know if my context is poisoned?
faq.answer: Watch for symptoms including degraded output quality on tasks that previously succeeded, tool misalignment where agents call wrong tools, and hallucinations that persist despite correction attempts.

faq.question: Which models handle long context best?
faq.answer: According to benchmarks, Claude Opus 4.5 shows degradation around 100K tokens, GPT-5.2 (thinking mode) around 64K, and Gemini 3 Pro around 500K. However, benchmarks vary by task type.
